body:
Title: Revenu Quebec Schedule D 2025 solidarity tax credit.

Source:
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/en/online-services/forms-and-publications/current-details/tp-1-v/
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/documents/en/formulaires/tp/2025-12/TP-1.D.D-V%282025-12%29.pdf

Schedule D numeric source values for encoded eligibility thresholds and definition limits:
Value: 18
Value: 183
Value: 90
Value: 12
Value: 60
Value: 3

Schedule D states that only one spouse completes the schedule if, on December 31, 2025, the taxpayer had a spouse who lived with the taxpayer; if the spouse did not live with the taxpayer, each spouse completes a separate Schedule D. The schedule is completed based on the taxpayer's situation on December 31, 2025. Revenu Quebec also takes into account the number of children under 18 for whom the taxpayer or spouse received the Family Allowance, using information obtained from Retraite Quebec.

Schedule D eligibility requirements state that the taxpayer may be eligible if, on December 31, 2025, the taxpayer was 18 or older, or if younger than 18 had a spouse, was the mother or father of a child who lived with the taxpayer, or was recognized as an emancipated minor by a competent authority. The taxpayer must have been resident in Quebec. The taxpayer or spouse must have been a Canadian citizen, a permanent resident or protected person, or a temporary resident or temporary resident permit holder who had been living in Canada for the last 18 months.

Schedule D exclusions state that the taxpayer is not eligible if confined to prison or a similar institution on December 31, 2025 and confined in 2025 for one or more periods totalling more than 183 days; if Retraite Quebec made a Family Allowance payment with regard to the taxpayer for December 2025 unless the taxpayer turned 18 during that month; or if the taxpayer and spouse, if applicable, were refugee protection claimants on December 31, 2025. The spouse definition excludes a person from whom the taxpayer has been living separate and apart for 90 days or more because of relationship breakdown. A de facto spouse includes a person living in a conjugal relationship with the taxpayer and a shared child, or a person living in a conjugal relationship for at least 12 consecutive months. Schedule D line 12 asks whether the taxpayer lived alone throughout 2025, treating the taxpayer as living alone if everyone the taxpayer lived with was under 18. Tenant and owner information is required for eligible dwellings, including RL-31 or municipal tax bill information where applicable. Rooms in certain rooming arrangements are ineligible unless at least three rooms are rented or offered for rent or the room has a separate outside entrance or separate sanitary facilities. Hotel or rooming house rooms leased or subleased for fewer than 60 consecutive days are not eligible dwellings.
